goal: instantiate Claude Code build-continuity + supervised-resume system in the repo

failure_modes: auto-mode classifier blocked a folderOpen+ExecutionPolicy-Bypass task
fixes: made the VS Code task safe+manual; documented opt-in for auto-run rather than silently weakening guardrails
lessons: never auto-install a guardrail-weakening persistence hook; the project's own trust model forbids it. Trust the CODE for status (~75-80%), not the blueprint's 35%.

goal: resumable in-chat YELLOW approval
plan: backend approved_commands allowlist + human_required event reusing executor.execute_approved; minimal frontend resend-on-approve

locked design into RESUME.md before /compact

goal: resumable in-chat YELLOW approval (chat pauses on a YELLOW command, then runs it after human approval)
plan: backend approved_commands allowlist + human_required pause reusing executor.execute_approved; map to the existing SSE human_required frame; minimal frontend resend-on-approve via an extracted streamGenerate
actions: tool_agent: __init__ approved_commands set; _format_exec_result helper; _execute returns new 'approval' status for an unauthorised YELLOW, or runs via execute_approved when whitelisted
run(): status=='approval' -> yield human_required + return (pause the turn)
main.py: GenerateRequest.approvedCommands; pass to ToolAgent; map agent human_required -> SSE human_required
tests: 3 tool_agent (pause / approved-runs / RED-refused-even-if-approved) + 2 api (pause / approved-runs)
frontend: approvedCommands state; extracted streamGenerate(history,approvedCmds); handleApproveAction whitelists + resumes the turn; handleSendMessage resets the whitelist
outcome: success: 94 passed, 1 skipped (was 89); eslint clean; vite build ok
lessons: reuse executor.execute_approved so human approval can never authorise RED (it still returns BLOCKED); resume a paused turn by replaying convHistory (which ends at the user msg, since the paused assistant turn records no answer) with the command whitelisted, rather than running it out-of-band via /api/terminal — this keeps the chat transcript coherent and lets the command's outcome still be reflected on

goal: fix two cosmetic glitches surfaced by the first live run of the 4h approval card
plan: clean the human-facing approval text + stop the perpetual tool_call spinner on a paused/settled turn
actions: live run showed the approval card, but the explanation displayed the raw classifier regex pattern (\bpip\s+install\b)
main.py human_required SSE now surfaces the command in plain language, never classify()'s raw reason (that pattern belongs in the audit log)
MessageBubble tool_call spinner was unconditional -> made it a static dot once the message is 'settled'
App.jsx marks the message settled:true on done/human_required/error
test_api asserts the friendly prompt is present AND the raw 'Caution operation requires approval' reason never leaks
outcome: success: 94 passed / 1 skipped; eslint+build clean; approval card now reads cleanly
failure_modes: unit tests asserted the human_required event fired but not the human-readability of its text, so the regex leak slipped through to the live UI
fixes: added a presentation-layer assertion (no raw classifier reason in the SSE body)
lessons: a passing 'event emitted' test is not a passing 'UI is readable' test; when surfacing an internal reason string to a human, assert on its content and keep regex/pattern internals in the audit log, not the prompt

goal: the approval card's Run/Reject buttons were clipped below the panel fold so the turn looked stuck at 'Awaiting approval'
plan: auto-scroll the chat panel when the approval card appears
actions: chatEndRef auto-scroll only fired on messages change, not when pendingAction (the tall approval card) appeared
added a pendingAction-triggered effect that scrolls chatEndRef into view after a 60ms layout delay
outcome: success: eslint+build clean; Run/Reject buttons now revealed when the card appears
failure_modes: a tall conditionally-rendered element (the approval card) appeared without an accompanying scroll, leaving its action buttons off-screen
fixes: scroll on the element's appearance, not only on list changes
lessons: when a new interactive element can push key controls below the fold, trigger a scroll on THAT element's mount, with a small delay so its layout is measured first; the user's 'still same issue' was a different problem (clipped buttons) than the cosmetics I'd just fixed — re-diagnose from the screenshot, don't assume

goal: the approval card's Run/Reject buttons were clipped below the fold inside the scrollable chat; auto-scroll did not reliably reveal them
plan: stop fighting scroll - pin the approval as a flex-shrink-0 action bar OUTSIDE the message scroll list, above the composer, so its controls are structurally always visible
actions: moved the pendingAction card out of the overflowY:auto messages div to a sibling between the list and the input composer
redesigned it premium: glassmorphism, slide-up entrance (approvalIn), ambient amber glow (approvalGlow), sweeping accent (sweep), gradient Run button with hover-lift + press feedback, YELLOW badge
added 3 keyframes to index.css; removed the now-pointless pendingAction scroll effect
outcome: success: eslint+build clean; approval controls can no longer be clipped regardless of scroll
failure_modes: two prior attempts (cosmetic text fix, then scroll-into-view) treated symptoms; the real fault was layout - a blocking control rendered inside a scroll region
fixes: render blocking/critical controls OUTSIDE scroll containers as pinned siblings
lessons: never place a required decision (approval) inside a scrollable log; pin it. When the user says 'still same problem' after a fix, the fix addressed the wrong layer - re-diagnose structurally instead of iterating on the same layer

goal: the chat UI was broken by a parallel premium-2026 rewrite (rewritten MessageBubble/LivePreview with incompatible props + new styles/*.css imported into App.jsx/index.css) that landed in the working tree alongside my edits
plan: per user choice (stabilize then polish): restore the working components + clean CSS baseline, park the new CSS unimported, then polish incrementally
actions: caught it because git status showed files I never edited (LivePreview, MessageBubble) modified + untracked styles/*; diffed them
my commit c500ed0 had unknowingly absorbed 3 foreign style-import lines because they were already in App.jsx/index.css when I edited those files

git restore MessageBubble+LivePreview to HEAD (working {msg} contract); git restore index.css to HEAD~1 (clean baseline, since the foreign edit also deleted 13 original lines) then re-applied my approval keyframes; removed the 3 foreign imports from App.jsx
left styles/{App,design-system,nexgen-3d,nexgen-layout}.css untracked+unimported to preserve the user work
outcome: success: eslint+build clean; app contract restored; pinned approval bar retained
failure_modes: staged files with git add <file> that contained concurrent foreign edits -> absorbed foreign import lines into my commit without noticing
fixes: always git diff <file> before git add when the tree may contain others concurrent edits; reconcile by restoring to a known-good ref + re-applying only my hunks
lessons: in a shared working tree treat every file as possibly co-edited: read the diff before staging, and prefer restoring to a known-good commit over surgically removing foreign lines when the foreign edit also deleted content

goal: add AWS Bedrock as a cloud LLM provider so the agentic loop can run in the cloud when the local 4GB GPU cannot host a model (Ollama CUDA-OOM)
plan: a BedrockClient implementing the same chat(messages,tools,model) contract as OllamaClient via the Converse API; provider routing in /api/generate; creds from env only; tested with a fake boto3 client
actions: config.py: BEDROCK_REGION/MODEL/MAX_TOKENS/ENABLED (env-driven; disabled unless region+model both set)

aios/core/bedrock.py: BedrockClient + _to_converse (pairs Ollama-style tool_calls/tool msgs into Converse toolUse/toolResult by minted ids) + _to_tool_config + _parse_output; lazy boto3 import; failures -> LLMError
main.py: get_bedrock_client dep + _select_chat_client routing (ollama.* -> local; else -> Bedrock on config.BEDROCK_MODEL when available, else local fallback); /api/generate uses the chosen client
tests: 8 in test_bedrock.py + 1 api routing test, all with injected fakes (no live AWS / no boto3 needed)
added boto3 stack to requirements via pip freeze
outcome: success: 103 passed, 1 skipped (was 94)
lessons: the agents Ollama-shaped protocol (tool_calls without ids; role:tool results) needs careful stateful conversion to Converse id-paired toolUse/toolResult; keep boto3 a lazy import so the cloud provider stays optional and tests need neither boto3 nor AWS creds. local-first stays default; cloud is opt-in via AIOS_BEDROCK_REGION + AIOS_BEDROCK_MODEL.

goal: let the dropdown run any Bedrock LLM (Claude/Nova/Llama/...) the account can invoke, not just one configured default
plan: backend list_models discovery + endpoint + route the selected id through to Converse; frontend Cloud (Bedrock) group from discovery with curated fallback
actions: bedrock.py: BedrockClient.list_models() via control-plane ListFoundationModels (TEXT, ON_DEMAND), dedup+sort, injectable ctrl_client for tests, []-on-error so a key without control-plane access degrades gracefully
main.py: /api/v1/models/bedrock endpoint; _select_chat_client now passes the selected model id through to Bedrock (each dropdown choice runs that model)
frontend: bedrockStatus probe + Cloud (Bedrock) group (falls back to the static curated list when discovery is empty) + PROVIDER_META entry
tests: +4 (list_models filter/sort/dedup + error; endpoint lists + empty)
outcome: success: 107 passed, 1 skipped; eslint+build clean
failure_modes: pytest CRASHED with a Windows access-violation in torch/transformers — live app usage had populated the on-disk FAISS index, so /api/generate tests fell through _recall_memory -> hybrid_search and loaded the REAL sentence-transformers embedder (a model side-effect in a test path, which segfaults on this box)
fixes: stubbed aios.api.main.hybrid_search -> [] in the client fixture (recall tests override via the same monkeypatch); restores green AND isolates tests from live data, per the project rule of no model/network/shell side-effects in tests
lessons: when a project shares one DATA_DIR between the live app and tests, live usage can leak state into tests and trip code paths that were only safe while the index was empty; stub recall/model-loading in the fixture so tests never depend on on-disk state. ABSK Bedrock API keys may not grant control-plane (ListFoundationModels) access -> always provide a curated fallback for discovery.

goal: wire the existing Verifier into the live agentic loop (/api/generate) so the agent can PROVE its work, closing execute->verify->reflect

_dispatch routes verify -> _verify(command) calling .verify(command,session_id=self.session_id)

lessons: reuse the existing component's own reflection hook and gate the loop's reflect path by tool name (only execute_terminal reflects) to avoid double-reflection; a verify tool needs NO new event type - tool_result/tool_blocked already flow as `step` SSE, so frontend work is one TOOL_META line; before claiming a regression, stash and re-run to separate environmental failures from your own

actions: added plan entry to TOOL_SPECS (one `goal` param) + module-docstring tool list
import LLMClient + Planner/PlannerError; __init__ takes planner_llm: Optional[LLMClient], builds self._planner=Planner(planner_llm) once (None when absent)

_dispatch routes plan -> _plan(goal): None->[plan unavailable]; PlannerError->[plan error]; success->ordered steps w/ confidences + human-review section when requires_human; always status ok, failed False (advisory, never reflected)

added 4 tests + a FakePlannerLLM (.complete) fake: lists steps, flags <0.72 step, PlannerError clean (no reflect), graceful unavailable + done
outcome: success: +4 passing tests (45/45 tool_agent+verifier+planner); full backend 152 passed/4 skipped with the 2 PRE-EXISTING environmental test_security.py fails (confirmed identical with changes stashed); frontend vitest 9/9 + build + eslint green

fixes: filter by type=='tool_result' (mirror the verify tests) before indexing output
lessons: CRITICAL: planner needs a COMPLETION client (.complete); the loop's chat client may be cloud Bedrock with no .complete() — inject the completion client, never reuse self.llm; advisory tools should surface bad input as a normal status-ok tool_result (NOT a security block, NOT a reflectable failure); when asserting on a tool's result event, filter by type=='tool_result' because both tool_call and tool_result carry the same `tool` name; squash-merged PRs leave the local feature branch off origin/master — reset --hard onto origin/master before the next increment so the new PR diff is clean

FINDING in review: _plan caught only PlannerError but Planner.plan calls planner_llm.complete which raises LLMError if the local model is down; run() does not wrap _dispatch so that would abort the turn

goal: clear AUDIT Tier-2 #3 (rollback git repo lived inside the main-repo-tracked training_ground/) and #4 (tests ran against the live data/ DBs+FAISS index) in ONE focused PR, as two separable changes
plan: .aios/state/AUDIT.md Tier-2 + RESUME next-action: #3 relocate the rollback git DATABASE under a new gitignored config.ROLLBACK_DIR (separate_git_dir) so the sandbox stays the work-tree but no .git DB lands in-tree; gitignore the residual pointer + .gitkeep. #4 add tests/conftest.py that sets AIOS_DATA_DIR to a tempfile.mkdtemp at module level BEFORE aios.config imports, isolating all derived paths; the empty temp index lets hybrid_search short-circuit so the client-fixture stub could be dropped.
actions: added config.ROLLBACK_DIR (=_env_path(AIOS_ROLLBACK_DIR, DATA_DIR/'rollback')) + __all__

RollbackEngine: added git_dir param; default sandbox uses Repo.init(work_tree, separate_git_dir=ROLLBACK_DIR); injected repo_dir keeps DB in-tree; _ensure_repo treats a .git pointer FILE or dir as 'already init' and reopens via work-tree
gitignored training_ground/.git + training_ground/.gitkeep
added tests/conftest.py (module-level AIOS_DATA_DIR tempdir + atexit cleanup)
removed the now-unneeded hybrid_search stub from tests/test_api.py client fixture
added tests/test_data_isolation.py (3) + 3 rollback tests
verified production path: training_ground/.git is a 43-byte pointer, real DB under data/rollback/, git status clean (git check-ignore confirms)
ran full suite + stashed-diff comparison for the 2 pre-existing security fails
outcome: success: full suite 159 passed / 4 skipped / 2 failed; the 2 fails are the pre-existing+environmental test_security.py cases (verified identical with changes stashed). Rollback snapshot/rollback + project-root refusal intact; live data/ untouched.
lessons: GitPython separate_git_dir cleanly moves a git DB out of a tracked work-tree, leaving only a small 'gitdir:' POINTER file; treat either a .git dir OR a .git pointer file as 'repo already initialised' and always reopen via the work-tree path so the pointer is followed transparently. For test isolation of import-time-derived config paths, set the env var at conftest.py MODULE level (pytest imports conftest before test modules, hence before the first aios.config import) rather than in a fixture. An isolated empty FAISS index makes hybrid_search short-circuit without loading the embedder, which let a long-standing test stub be removed and the no-model-in-tests contract be pinned by an explicit test instead.

goal: build the READ-ONLY foundation (Tiers T0+T1) of the marquee Self-Analysis module per Assessment §6 — index + diagnose the system's OWN aios/ code, write a structured report, wire a read-only self_analyze tool — strictly GREEN (no source edits, no exec, no model)
plan: aiosv6_assessment_text.md §6 (design + §6.4 report schema + §6.5 T0/T1 stub): new aios/agents/self_analysis_agent.py (stdlib ast/pathlib/hashlib/re only); add self_analysis_report table to schema.sql via the idempotent IF NOT EXISTS pattern; wire a self_analyze tool into tool_agent.py mirroring verify/plan, path confined via the existing _resolve_within(read_root,...) resolver; one frontend TOOL_META entry; tests over a fixture tree
actions: wrote SelfAnalysisAgent: T0 scan_module->ModuleFacts (rel path, loc, funcs/classes via ast.walk, imports + intra-package edges) + build_map; T1 diagnose -> missing_test/smell/todo/complexity Findings; analyze() pure (no DB/no write), write_report()/read_findings() persist via init_memory_db+get_connection

added self_analysis_report table + idx_sar_status/idx_sar_path to aios/memory/schema.sql per §6.4 (status CHECK incl open/proposed/approved/applied/rolled_back/rejected; llm/proposed/applied cols NULLable)

wired self_analyze TOOL_SPECS entry (optional path, default aios) + _dispatch route + _self_analyze (confines path via _resolve_within, runs agent, writes report, returns summary; status ok/failed False)
added 🔬 self_analyze TOOL_META entry to MessageBubble.jsx
wrote tests/test_self_analysis.py (7): T0 map, T1 todo+line/smell/missing_test, long-function smell, write->read rows, source-hash-unchanged, tool summary, path-escape refused

verified: new tests 7/7; full suite 166 passed/4 skipped/2 failed (the 2 are pre-existing env test_security.py, identical with changes stashed); frontend eslint+vitest 9/9+build green; real-path smoke over live aios/ = 31 modules/41 findings/65 import edges
outcome: success: read-only T0/T1 foundation built, tested, and wired into the live loop; +7 tests (159->166 passing on Linux). Proven on the real codebase, never touches source.
lessons: For a deterministic static analyzer, keep analyze() PURE (no DB, no side effects) and make persistence a separate explicit call — it makes the agent, the table, and the tool wiring independently testable and lets the read-only guarantee be proven by hashing the tree before/after. Reuse the agent's existing read-side resolver (_resolve_within(read_root, path)) for a new read-only tool instead of inventing a new scope check — confinement stays consistent with read_file and there's nothing new to get wrong. A self-contained DB writer should ensure its schema via the idempotent init_memory_db rather than assuming a prior bootstrap, so it works on a fresh/temp DB (incl. the isolated test DATA_DIR). The simple test-by-convention rule (test_<stem>.py) is intentionally coarse — it even flags self_analysis_agent.py because its test is test_self_analysis.py — which is fine for T1: deterministic-but-coarse now, refined later by the coverage.py join (left as a TODO).

actions: gh pr checkout 4; full suite 171 passed/1 skipped on Windows (164 baseline + 7 new)

read the diff + verified _resolve_within (tool_agent.py:263) fail-closes on ../ /abs/symlink and is the SAME resolver read_file/_read_directory use; get_connection commits on success; init_memory_db idempotent

independent real-tree smoke: hashed every aios/**.py before+after analyze()+write_report() to a TEMP db -> REAL_SOURCE_UNCHANGED True; reproduced 31 modules / 41 findings {missing_test 20, complexity 11, smell 5, todo 5} / 65 import edges exactly
frontend eslint clean + vitest 9/9 + vite build green
conflict was RESUME.md only (master 6eff1aa refresh vs branch edit); merged origin/master INTO the branch, took --ours for RESUME.md, pushed (no force)
gh pr ready 4 (it was a draft) then gh pr merge 4 --squash --delete-branch -> 4cb01b6 on master; pulled; re-ran 171/1 green on master
outcome: success: PR #4 squash-merged to master (4cb01b6); the marquee Self-Analysis module's read-only T0/T1 foundation is on master, suite 171/1; reviewed correct as-submitted, no patch needed
failure_modes: GitHub reported the PR CONFLICTING/DIRTY (brain-file RESUME.md only) and gh pr merge first refused because the PR was still a DRAFT
fixes: resolved RESUME.md by merging master into the branch with --ours (it is overwritten at closeout anyway); marked the PR ready with gh pr ready before merging
lessons: Review on evidence, not the PR body: re-running on the real Windows baseline turned the body's reported '2 failed' (Linux-only environmental security cases) into a clean 0-failed, and an independent before/after SHA-256 hash of the LIVE aios/ tree proved the read-only claim on real code, not just the fixture. A draft PR must be gh pr ready before gh pr merge. When a cloud PR conflicts only on brain files, merge master INTO the branch and take --ours rather than force-pushing a rebase. Carry into T2: write_report always INSERTs findings as open, so repeated self_analyze runs accumulate duplicate rows — add a scan/run grouping or de-dup before T2 turns findings into proposals.

updated RESUME (two tracks) + CEO_LOG; pushed brain 6615717 then 0cb4beb
outcome: partial: planning + specs + staging done and on origin; no code merged yet (awaits operator launching ultracode and the Ollama run)
lessons: Two distinct gates: external engineers (Claude Code + ultracode) edit aios/ behind the supervised PR gate, but the PRODUCT agent is bound by config.SCOPE_ROOTS = training_ground only, so editing its own aios/ source auto-escalates to RED and is refused BY DESIGN. So self-improvement on its own code IS the guarded T2+ path, not a quick first breath; the available first breath is in the sandbox. Also edit_file is search/replace and cannot create or write an empty file, so authoring new files needs a new create_file tool. When two ultracode tasks touch the same module, sequence them (merge one, rebase the next) to keep PR diffs clean.

goal: implement .aios/state/ULTRACODE_TASK.md (a): replace self_analysis_report's plain INSERT-per-finding write_report with a fingerprint-based reconcile so re-runs stop accumulating duplicate 'open' rows (pre-T2 data-model hygiene)
plan: spec was fully prescriptive; implement exactly, flag don't silently change. Add symbol to Finding + finding_fingerprint(path,type,symbol); rewrite write_report as a scope-confined reconcile returning ReconcileResult (insert new / update open / skip decided / close vanished); add fingerprint column to schema.sql; add idempotent _migrate in db.py (ALTER ADD COLUMN + drop legacy open NULL-fp rows + UNIQUE partial index on open fingerprint); update _self_analyze summary; update + add tests
actions: Finding gained symbol:str='' (default keeps positional 3-arg calls valid); construction sites pass symbol = func name (long-func smell, complexity) / trimmed TODO line text (todo) / '' (missing_test, module smell)
added module-level finding_fingerprint = sha256(path \x1f type \x1f symbol) + frozen ReconcileResult{inserted,updated,closed,skipped,open_total}

rewrote write_report: init+migrate, derive prefix=self._rel(scope_root), load existing rows under (target_path=prefix OR LIKE prefix+'/%'), build open_by_fp + decided_fps, then per fresh fp skip/update/insert, then delete vanished open rows, then COUNT open in scope
schema.sql: fingerprint TEXT after evidence (nullable, NULL on legacy)

db.py: init_memory_db now executescript THEN _migrate(conn); _migrate adds the column on legacy DBs, deletes legacy status=open AND fingerprint IS NULL rows, creates UNIQUE INDEX idx_sar_open_fp WHERE status='open'
tool_agent _self_analyze: summary uses ReconcileResult (open_total/inserted/closed), kept the two pinned substrings

tests: updated test_write_report_persists_open_findings (ReconcileResult + fingerprint not null); +6 (idempotent rerun, no-reopen-decided, close-vanished, fingerprint-stable-when-todo-moves, scope-confined, legacy-db migration)
outcome: success: full suite 172 passed / 4 skipped / 2 failed (the 2 are the known pre-existing/environmental test_security.py cases, identical with changes stashed). Real-path smoke over live aios/: run1 inserted 45, run2 inserted 0 / updated 45, 45 unique fingerprints = 45 rows — duplicate accumulation gone.
lessons: For a re-runnable analyzer that persists findings, give each finding a STABLE line-number-free fingerprint (path+type+symbol, NOT evidence) and make the writer a reconcile, not an INSERT: update open / skip decided / close vanished. Enforce 'one open row per fingerprint' with a SQLite UNIQUE PARTIAL INDEX (WHERE status='open') so duplicates are impossible at the DB level, not just in code. Add post-release columns via an idempotent _migrate (ALTER TABLE) AFTER executescript — CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS never alters an existing table; and create the partial index in _migrate (not schema.sql) so it only runs once the column is guaranteed present on both fresh and migrated DBs. Two faithful-to-spec flags worth catching by reading carefully: Path.relative_to(self)=='.' not '' (so the spec's prefix=='' whole-table guard needed prefix in ('','.')), and an unescaped LIKE treats '_'/'%' as wildcards (harmless for 'aios'/'pkg' scopes, noted as a v1 caveat).

gh pr ready 5 then gh pr merge 5 squash delete-branch to 17d96f5 on master; pulled; re-ran 177 and 1 green; restored the seed pair; deleted the consumed ULTRACODE_TASK.md
outcome: success: PR #5 merged to master (17d96f5), suite 177 and 1; the report table now de-dups by fingerprint so T2 can turn open findings into proposals without duplicate or ambiguous rows. Correct as-submitted, no patch (like PR 3 and 4).
lessons: With NO testpaths configured pytest collects from the rootdir recursively, so an in-repo demo seed test that fails by design will pollute a suite run — park it first or set testpaths. Reviewing on evidence paid off again: re-running on Windows turned the Linux 2-failed into 0, and an independent before-after hash of the live aios tree plus a twice-run reconcile proved both the read-only guarantee and the no-dup-accumulation claim on real code. When a cloud PR brain-file change is a clean append on top of your latest rather than a stale overwrite, let it merge instead of neutralizing.

goal: implement .aios/state/ULTRACODE_NEXT_create_file.md: add a gated create_file tool so the agent can author NEW files in its sandbox (edit_file can only modify existing files), behind the SAME human gate as edit_file
plan: mirror edit_file exactly, do NOT invent a new security path. Backend: TOOL_SPECS create_file + _dispatch + _create_file (scope-lock via is_path_in_scope, refuse existing, pause YELLOW with all-additions diff, snapshot+audit fail-closed then write) + approved_creations channel. API: approvedCreations request field + ToolAgent passthrough + human_required creation branch. Frontend: approvedCreations state + streamGenerate + approve/reject/send wiring + DiffView reuse + TOOL_META. Tests mirror edit_file.
actions: tool_agent.py: added create_file TOOL_SPECS (filepath, content) + module docstring bullet; __init__ approved_creations (filepath->content map, keyed by filepath for drift-robust resume); _dispatch route; run() approval branch sets event creation+diff; new _create_file mirroring _edit_file (resolve_within + is_path_in_scope, refuse exists -> points to edit_file, all-additions difflib.unified_diff([], content, /dev/null -> b/path), pause if unapproved, else snapshot(fail-closed)+audit(fail-closed)+mkdir(parents within scope)+write_text)
api/main.py: GenerateRequest.approved_creations (alias approvedCreations) -> ToolAgent(approved_creations=...); human_required handler creation branch builds input.creations + diff payload

frontend App.jsx: approvedCreations state; streamGenerate 4th arg + body field; handleApproveAction reads pendingAction.creations and threads newApprovedCreations; reject + send reset it; approve button label 'Create file'
MessageBubble.jsx: TOOL_META create_file (🆕) + edit_file (✏️); DiffView.test.jsx: +all-additions test

tests/test_tool_agent.py: +8 (pause-with-preview, apply-approved+snapshot-despite-drift, audit-applied, refuse-existing->edit_file, out-of-scope blocked, ../ escape blocked, fail-closed on audit, fail-closed on snapshot)
outcome: success: full suite 180 passed / 4 skipped / 2 failed (the 2 = known pre-existing env test_security.py, identical with changes stashed). +8 backend create tests, frontend eslint clean + vitest 10/10 (+1 DiffView) + build green. Smoke: GenerateRequest(approvedCreations) + ToolAgent(approved_creations) + create_file in TOOL_SPECS all wire cleanly.
lessons: When adding a sibling capability (create_file next to edit_file), MIRROR the existing gated path exactly rather than inventing a new security route: reuse _resolve_within + is_path_in_scope, the snapshot-then-audit-then-write fail-closed ordering, and the approved_* filepath-keyed channel (so resume applies exactly what the human approved, not the model's drifted regen). is_path_in_scope works on non-existent targets (Path.resolve(strict=False)), so the same scope check covers a not-yet-created file. An all-additions preview is just difflib.unified_diff([], content.splitlines(keepends=True), fromfile='/dev/null', tofile=f'b/{path}') — DiffView already colours '+' lines, so the UI is free. Snapshot 'before' = file absent means an auto-rollback correctly deletes the new file (clean T3 story). Reset every approved_* whitelist on a new user message to keep the per-request approval boundary tight.

frontend eslint clean, vitest 10 of 10 (added a DiffView all-additions test), vite build green

independent adversarial probe under the real scope training_ground: create_file to aios slash evil, to dotdot slash evil, and to an absolute C drive path were ALL blocked with written False even with an approval supplied; only an in-sandbox path wrote — confirming the gate refuses self-edits in the real config not just the monkeypatched test

gh pr ready 6 then gh pr merge 6 squash delete-branch to 02f93cc on master; pulled; re-ran 185 and 1 green; restored the seed pair; deleted the consumed ULTRACODE_NEXT_create_file.md
checkpointed RESUME: create_file MERGED, next BUILD is (b) coverage radon behind a deps-OK decision gate
outcome: success: PR #6 merged to master (02f93cc), suite 185 and 1; the agent can now author NEW files in its sandbox behind the same human gate as edit_file, with the scope boundary independently proven. Correct as-submitted, no patch (like PR 3, 4, 5).
lessons: For a new WRITE capability, do not just trust the PR security tests — run an INDEPENDENT adversarial probe under the REAL scope config (not the test monkeypatch): construct the agent with the default SCOPE_ROOTS and confirm out-of-sandbox, dotdot escape, and absolute targets are all refused with nothing written EVEN WHEN an approval is supplied. Mirroring an existing gated path (create_file from edit_file) keeps the security review tractable: same resolver, same scope check, same snapshot-then-audit-then-write fail-closed order, same filepath-keyed approval channel. A good sibling PR also tightens what it touches — resetting all approved whitelists on a new user message closed a latent gap where approvedEdits could linger across turns.

goal: implement .aios/state/ULTRACODE_TASK.md (b): sharpen Self-Analysis T1 before T2 — replace the AST branch-count complexity proxy with radon real cyclomatic complexity, and add a read-only coverage join (new 'uncovered' finding for testable modules the suite never executed)
plan: fail-soft optional deps (project lazy-dep style); radon cc_visit in _scan_source_findings (keep proxy as fallback); coverage join in diagnose via _measured_files reading an existing .coverage; analyze() stays pure; fingerprint-stable complexity symbol = bare block.name; NO tool_agent/frontend/security change; add radon to requirements
actions: added try/except imports for radon.complexity.cc_visit and coverage (degrade if absent) + import os

_complexity_findings(src,tree,rel_path): radon cc_visit flat list, keep letter in (F,M), symbol=bare block.name, evidence 'cyclomatic complexity N (> t)'; per-file try/except falls back to _complexity_proxy_findings (the old branch-count, kept); _BRANCH_NODES retained as fallback

diagnose: __init__ coverage_data_path; _measured_files() reads existing .coverage (CoverageData.read -> realpath set) fail-soft -> None; testable module realpath not in measured -> Finding 'uncovered'; dormant when no coverage/file
schema.sql finding_type comment + module/diagnose docstrings updated; TODO(radon)/TODO(coverage) resolved; dead_code noted deferred (needs vulture)
requirements.txt += radon==6.0.1 and (flagged) transitive mando==0.7.1; installed both in venv

tests/test_self_analysis.py +5: radon real metric, proxy fallback (monkeypatch _radon_cc_visit=None), coverage uncovered flags unmeasured/not-measured, dormant without data, diagnose-with-coverage read-only
verified full suite 185 passed/4 skipped/2 failed (2 = pre-existing env test_security, identical stashed); real-path smoke over live aios -> 11 cyclomatic-complexity findings, bare symbols, no crash
outcome: success: sharper T1 diagnosis (real radon CC + uncovered join) landed read-only and fail-soft; +5 tests (180->185). No tool_agent/frontend/security change.
lessons: radon.complexity.cc_visit returns a FLAT list where methods are flattened to top-level alongside their Class object — filter block.letter in ('F','M') (skip 'C') and DON'T recurse into Class.methods or you double-count. Keep the symbol = bare block.name so a metric swap (proxy->radon) UPDATES existing open fingerprinted rows instead of duplicating them — fingerprint stability is a cross-implementation contract, not just cross-run. Optional analysis deps belong behind try/except with a real fallback (radon->AST proxy) or a dormant no-op (coverage absent), so the module imports and the suite runs degraded. A coverage 'never-executed' join is cheap + deterministic by READING an existing .coverage (CoverageData.read + measured_files, realpath both sides) — never run coverage/tests inside a read-only analyzer. When a spec says 'add one dep' but the repo pins ALL transitive deps in a flat requirements.txt, pin the transitive dep too (mando for radon) and flag it — an incomplete pin list silently breaks reproducible installs.

investigated a suspicious 24 uncovered on live aios: the root .coverage measures only one file (api main) and is gitignored, so the join faithfully flagged the rest from stale partial data — NOT a realpath bug (the one measured file is correctly excluded); confirmed by inspecting measured_files
gh pr ready 7 then gh pr merge 7 squash delete-branch to f1b5e36 on master; pulled; re-ran 190 and 1 green; restored the seed pair; wrote the (c) golden-harness spec into ULTRACODE_TASK.md
outcome: success: PR #7 merged to master (f1b5e36), suite 190 and 1; T1 complexity is now a real radon metric plus an uncovered join. Correct as-submitted, no patch (like PR 3 4 5 6).
lessons: To truly review an optional-dep feature, INSTALL the dep locally so its path actually runs instead of importorskip-ing past it. When a real-tree smoke shows a surprising number, INSPECT the data source before concluding bug-or-benign: the root .coverage had a single measured file, so the uncovered join was reflecting stale partial coverage, not mis-matching realpaths. A coverage never-executed join is only as good as the .coverage freshness — advise a full pytest --cov before trusting uncovered. Pin transitive deps when the project uses a flat fully-pinned requirements list.
